N01.label: likely_eligible

profile.Sleep problem: wakes at 3 a.m. and cannot get back to sleep, about 5 nights a week
profile.Duration: roughly 2 years, worse in the past six months

N01.rationale: 5 nights/week for 2 years, no exclusions. Baseline easy case.

N02.label: likely_eligible

profile.Sleep problem: trouble falling asleep about 4 nights a week
profile.Sleep apnea: YES - diagnosed obstructive sleep apnea 3 years ago, and he uses his CPAP every night, 7+ hours; his apnea is well controlled

N02.rationale: Signature trap: he HAS sleep apnea, but it is treated with nightly CPAP, which the protocol explicitly allows. Bots that exclude any apnea diagnosis get this wrong.

N03.label: likely_ineligible

profile.Sleep problem: trouble falling asleep 4-5 nights a week
profile.Duration: about 5 weeks - started right after a stressful job change

N03.rationale: Sleep problems for only 5 weeks - short of the 3-month chronicity requirement (INC3). Frequency qualifies; duration does not.

N04.label: likely_ineligible

profile.Sleep problem: wakes repeatedly, about 5 nights a week
profile.Sleep medications: takes trazodone; his doctor DOUBLED the dose 2 weeks ago to see if it helps

N04.rationale: Trazodone dose changed 2 weeks ago - inside the 4-week EXC3 window.

N05.label: likely_ineligible

profile.Sleep problem: trouble falling asleep and staying asleep, 4-6 nights a week

N05.rationale: Rotating shifts including overnights (EXC2) - CBT-I protocols assume a stable day-oriented schedule.

N06.label: likely_ineligible

profile.Sleep problem: trouble staying asleep 4 nights a week
profile.Sleep apnea: diagnosed obstructive sleep apnea 14 months ago; a CPAP was prescribed but he NEVER PICKED IT UP - 'it's still at the supply company, I keep meaning to deal with it'

N06.rationale: Diagnosed sleep apnea that is NOT currently treated (EXC1) - the mirror image of N02. The treated/untreated distinction is the whole test.

N07.label: likely_ineligible

profile.Sleep problem: trouble falling asleep about 2 nights a week - 'the odd bad night'
profile.Sleep medications: her doctor started her on zolpidem 1 week ago for those bad nights

N07.rationale: Multi-failure: only 2 affected nights/week (below the 3-night floor, INC2) AND started zolpidem 1 week ago (EXC3). Both must be listed.

N08.label: insufficient_information

profile.Sleep problem: sleeps badly, but the number of bad nights per week is UNKNOWN - 'honestly no idea, I don't keep track; some weeks are fine, some are a mess, I couldn't put a number on it'
profile.Duration: on and off for about 2 years

N08.rationale: Cannot estimate affected nights per week, so INC2 is unknown per the protocol rule. Duration is known (about 2 years), so INC3 passes and only INC2 is unknown.

N09.label: likely_eligible

profile.Sleep problem: trouble falling asleep about 4 nights a week

N09.rationale: Everything qualifies. The test is whether the bot explains question purposes briefly and reassures about data use (per its own rules) without getting derailed or skipping criteria.
